title: A site for my high school to access digital manuals.
snippet: It’s not properly a tutorial but it sets the way you can follow to do a thing that ressembles mine.This post explains how and why I made this site, and the main steps to do it yourself. The site is...
content: It’s not properly a tutorial but it sets the way you can follow to do a thing that ressembles mine.This post explains how and why I made this site, and the main steps to do it yourself. The site is in French as I am French, but feel free to translate to any language.1. Create a project, and choose how to style your site.First, create a HTML/CSS/JS project in your favourite IDE, or via an online service (as https://glitch.com).You can use Bootstrap for the graphics as it made many things (including CSS struggling) much much easier.You may start to design a first page, but keep in mind that we will use javascript to generate all the content automatticaly : we will only use an index.html page.You can also start wandering for links to the digital version of your manuals, in most of europe they are free to read online since covid.You can also wander for images of the covers, download them.2. Create a json file for all the data and parse it with javascript.The next step is to create a data.json file, that will store all the data of your site.I recommand you formatting it like this one :[    // One category for one level    {        "name": "name_of_your_class",        "title": "the title you want to display on the page",        "content":             [                {                    "name": "Name of your manual",                    "link": "Link to the page of the manual",                    "image": "Link to the cover of the manual"                },                 // repeat for all the manuals you want to add            ]    }    // repeat for all the classes you want to]You can then open it with a function (that is called on click on the buttons on the main page), and parse it as you want.

title: My actions with HEVE
snippet: This post summarizes my participation with Nikita Guerlet’s HEVE project. HEVE is a label meant to encourage little artists on creativity, social media…DetailsI am responsible of all the design and...
content: This post summarizes my participation with Nikita Guerlet’s HEVE project. HEVE is a label meant to encourage little artists on creativity, social media…DetailsI am responsible of all the design and the creation of the site, as I have enough capabilities to tackle that. Here’s a theoric timeline of the project:  Wordpress installed and working  Design and site content  Server renting for hosting  Success HavingMore detail directly on the site.

content: This post is to show Markdown syntax rendering on Chirpy, you can also use it as an example of writing. Maecenas interdum malesuada egestas. Duis consectetur porta risus, sit amet vulputate urna facilisis ac. Phasellus semper dui non purus ultrices sodales. Aliquam ante lorem, ornare a feugiat ac, finibus nec mauris. Vivamus ut tristique nisi. Sed vel leo vulputate, efficitur risus non, posuere mi. Nullam tincidunt bibendum rutrum. Proin commodo ornare sapien. Vivamus interdum diam sed sapien blandit, sit amet aliquam risus mattis. Nullam arcu turpis, mollis quis laoreet at, placerat id nibh.
